summary: Adult families who exit shelter with a housing subsidy are a very small group each year (well under 700 households), so this return-rate percentage is sensitive to just a few additional or fewer returning families year to year. DHS's Fiscal 2024 report explicitly notes that swings in adult-family placement figures are "partially explained by" the small size of this population, which is the most plausible driver of the up-and-down pattern here rather than a shift in program effectiveness.
